summary: Russia conducted its first rehearsal for the annual Victory Day parade on Red Square. The event remains drastically scaled down this year with no display of military hardware due to heightened Ukrainian attack threats.
contents:
# Russia rehearses scaled-down Victory Day parade without weaponry display

## Overview
Russia conducted its first rehearsal for the annual Victory Day parade on Red Square. The event remains drastically scaled down this year with no display of military hardware due to heightened Ukrainian attack threats.

## Details
The parade commemorates victory over Nazi Germany in World War Two. Past events featured intercontinental ballistic missiles and other weapons to rally support for the Ukraine war. Kremlin officials cited increased risks from Ukrainian drones and strikes as the reason for omitting weaponry. Rehearsal occurred on Wednesday amid the ongoing fifth-year conflict. No specific units or equipment remained shown in the rehearsal. President Putin has historically used the parade for war messaging.[3]

**Location**: Moscow, Russia

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @Minhdr18, which covers Chinese and Russian military hardware, weapons systems, defense technology. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Chinese and Russian military hardware will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
